title: Ankura Hospitals launches 120-bed super speciality hospital in Indore
summary: Ankura Hospitals for Women and Children has inaugurated its state-of-the-art 120-bed super speciality hospital in Vijay Nagar, Indore. Pushyamitra Bhargav, Mayor, Indore was present during the launch of the new hospital. The Indore facility marks the hospital...
thbAction: Map the news to THB's provider CRM for referral growth, patient lifecycle engagement, and multi-facility relationship management across hospital and provider networks.
contentHtml:
The Indore facility marks the hospital group's entry into Madhya Pradesh

Ankura Hospitals for Women and Children has inaugurated its state-of-the-art 120-bed super speciality hospital in Vijay Nagar, Indore.

Pushyamitra Bhargav, Mayor, Indore was present during the launch of the new hospital. 

The Indore facility marks the hospital group’s entry into Madhya Pradesh. With this addition, Ankura Hospitals now operates 18 centres across Telangana, Andhra Pradesh, Maharashtra, Odisha and Madhya Pradesh, with a total capacity of over 1,500 beds.

According to the hospital, the new facility has been established to address the demand for specialised maternal and child healthcare services in the region. It said that patients requiring care for high-risk pregnancies, premature newborns, congenital disorders and complex paediatric conditions often travel outside the state for treatment. The Indore hospital is intended to provide tertiary care services within the region.

The hospital offers services in obstetrics and gynaecology, high-risk pregnancy management, neonatology, Level III neonatal intensive care (NICU), paediatrics, paediatric surgery, paediatric intensive care (PICU), foetal medicine, fertility and IVF, gynaecological care and 24×7 emergency care. The facility also includes labour and delivery suites, modular operation theatres, diagnostic services and critical care units.

Speaking on the occasion, Dr Krishna Prasad Rao Vunnam, Founder, Ankura Hospitals for Women and Children, said, “The inauguration of our Indore hospital is an important milestone in Ankura’s growth journey. As one of India’s fastest-growing women and child healthcare networks with more than 14 hospitals, our vision is to make specialised healthcare accessible to every mother and every child. Indore is an important healthcare hub for Central India, and this expansion reflects our long-term commitment to bringing world-class maternity, neonatal and paediatric care closer to families. Through advanced technology, experienced specialists and evidence-based clinical practices, we aim to provide the highest standards of care while reducing the need for patients to travel to larger metropolitan cities for specialised treatment.”

The hospital brings together a multidisciplinary team of obstetricians, gynaecologists, neonatologists, paediatricians, paediatric surgeons, foetal medicine specialists, anaesthesiologists and critical care experts, supported by highly trained nurses, respiratory therapists and neonatal care professionals to provide comprehensive treatment for mothers and children.

The Indore facility is expected to cater to residents and patients from Ujjain, Dewas, Dhar, Ratlam, Mhow, Khandwa, Khargone, Neemuch, Mandsaur and other districts across Madhya Pradesh.
